sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The charcoal moved back from cols 6-9 to cols 10-13. So clicking the blue square at rows 44-47 (below the lower black bar) moves the charcoal RIGHT.
Summary so far:
- Blue at rows 36-39 (above lower black bar): moves charcoal LEFT
- Blue at rows 44-47 (below lower black bar): moves charcoal RIGHT
This is consistent with Level 1's mechanics, where the blue squares adjacent to the black bar moved the charcoal.
Now I need to figure out the goal. In Level 1, the goal was to move the charcoal to the leftmost position. In Level 2, maybe the goal is similar - move the charcoal to a specific position.
The charcoal is currently at cols 10-13. The light green strip is at cols 8-9. When the charcoal moved left to cols 6-9, it overlapped with the light green strip.
Maybe the goal is to move the charcoal to the rightmost position? Or to align it with something?
Let me try moving the charcoal all the way right to see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Blue at rows 44-47 moves RIGHT. Move charcoal all the way right
print("Moving charcoal all the way right...")
for i in range(5):
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 46, 'col': 2}])
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] == 16:
            cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
            print(f"  After click {i+1}: charcoal cols = {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Check final state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"\nCharcoal area (rows 50-58, cols 40-56):")
for r in range(50, 58):
    print(f"  {r}: '{ascii_lines[r][40:56]}'")

print(f"\nAll objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    print(f"  {n['color']}: {n['pixels']}px")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
